title: Cornish pasty
subtitle: A crimped shortcrust pastry case filled with beef, swede, potato, and onion
excerpt: A Cornish pasty is a D-shaped pastry case crimped along the top, filled with raw skirt steak, potato, swede, and onion, and baked until the pastry is golden and the filling has cooked in its own steam. The traditional filling is always raw and always goes in in layers: potato and swede first, then meat, then onion.
sourceNotes: The Cornish pasty is a traditional Cornish food with origins as a miners' lunch, documented in Cornish sources from the eighteenth century onward. The traditional specification (skirt beef, potato, swede, onion, crimped along the top) is the form here. Florence White, Good Things in England (1932), Project Gutenberg, documents the Cornish pasty and its significance.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze assembled and unbaked. Bake from frozen at 180°C fan for 70 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Assemble up to a day ahead and refrigerate unbaked. Bake on the day.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; start high to set the pastry, drop to 180°C after 20 minutes

content.text: A Cornish pasty is a shortcrust pastry case filled with thinly sliced raw beef, potato, swede, and onion, crimped along the top, and baked long and slow so the filling steams and braises inside the sealed pastry. The filling goes in raw; the pastry acts as the pot. The crimp must be tight or the pastry will open and the filling will dry out.

items.prepNote: trimmed and cut into 5 mm pieces or thin slices

content.text: Rub the cold lard and butter into the flour and salt until it resembles coarse breadcrumbs. Add the cold water and bring together into a firm dough. Do not overwork. Divide into 4 equal pieces, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Roll each piece of pastry into a round, about 25 cm across. On one half of each round, layer in order: potato, swede, beef, onion. Season generously at each layer with salt and pepper. Leave a 2 cm border around the edge. Fold the pastry over the filling to form a D shape. Pinch the edge firmly together and crimp by folding and twisting the edge in small overlapping sections. Place on a lined baking tray. Brush with beaten egg and cut a small steam vent near the top of the crimp.

Bake at 200°C for 20 minutes to set and colour the pastry, then drop to 180°C for a further 35 minutes. The pasties are done when deep golden brown and the filling inside can be pierced easily through the steam vent with a skewer. Rest for 10 minutes before eating; the filling inside will be very hot.
